channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sony WH-1000XM6 Headphones Leak: Import Documents Suggest Close Launch
item.description: Stay up-to-date with the latest news on Sony's upcoming WH-1000XM6 headphones, expected to launch soon in India and Vietnam.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Honor rebrands as an 'AI device ecosystem company' and commits to developing a 'super intelligent' smartphone
item.description: Honor has announced its new Alpha Plan strategy, which includes a pledge to develop a 'super intelligent' smartphone.
item.dc:creator: axel.metz@futurenet.com (Axel Metz), TechRadar

item.title: After rolling out Sora beyond the US, OpenAI plans to put the video AI tool inside ChatGPT
item.description: ChatGPT can already generate text and images, and it seems video will be added to the list eventually.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Everything leaving Netflix in March 2025 – catch Sixteen Candles, Mad Max: Fury Road, and more before they're gone
item.description: Netflix has started its spring cleaning, so here are all the movies and shows leaving Netflix in March 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Paramount Plus in March 2025
item.description: From Tarantino to Lynch, Paramount Plus is bringing some of the best Hollywood movies to the platform in March 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: De'Longhi's new bean-to-cup coffee machine could make you a milk-frothing maestro
item.description: Love a latte but struggle to make them at home? The De'Longhi La Specialista Touch might be the solution.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Intel’s anticipated $28 billion chip factories in Ohio are delayed until 2030
item.description: Intel‘s promised $28 billion chip fabrication plants in Ohio are facing further delays, with the first factory in New Albany expected to not be completed until 2030, local media outlet The Columbus Dispatch reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: The internet has suspicions about family vloggers fleeing California. Here’s why
item.description: An unsubstantiated online theory has recently taken hold, claiming that family vloggers are fleeing Los Angeles to escape newly introduced California laws designed to protect children featured in online content.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why Ethan Klein is feuding with r/Fauxmoi
item.description: Pop culture subreddit r/Fauxmoi is embroiled in an ongoing controversy with YouTuber and podcaster Ethan Klein. Klein first rose to internet fame through his YouTube channel, h3h3Productions, which he co-created with his wife, Hila Klein. The channel now boasts 5.71 million subscribers.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Energy Department scientists participate in 'AI Jam' with OpenAI, Anthropic
item.description: Nearly 1,000 scientists from multiple U.S. National Laboratories gathered Friday to test artificial intelligence (AI) models from leading firms like OpenAI and Anthropic in an effort to harness the advancing technology for science and national security purposes.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Hollywood’s obsession with AI-enabled ‘perfection’ is making movies less human
item.description: The notion of authenticity in the movies has moved a step beyond the merely realistic. More and more, expensive and time-consuming fixes to minor issues of screen realism have become the work of statistical data renderings—the visual or aural products of generative artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: A.S. Hamrah, Fast Company

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google co-founder: Be in the office every weekday, work 60 hours a week
item.description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin sent an internal message Wednesday to the group working on the company’s AI model Gemini. In the message, Brin wrote that Google can become a leader in AI development — provided that employees work more.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google’s AI summaries are changing search. Now it’s facing a lawsuit
item.description: For more than two decades, users have turned to search engines like Google, typed in a query, and received a familiar list of 10 blue links—the gateway to the wider web. Ranking high on that list, through search engine optimization (SEO), has become a $200 billion business.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Washington Post loses 75K subscribers after Bezos-ordered op-ed pivot
item.description: The Washington Post has reportedly lost tens of thousands of subscribers in an apparent reaction to a decision by the outlet's billionaire owner to change the editorial focus of its opinion pages.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: How to avoid the hidden costs of onboarding
item.description: For employees, onboarding shapes their first impression of the company, helping them feel welcome and confident in their decision to join. For employers, onboarding involves numerous costs – both monetary and less quantifiable – that can make or break the bottom line.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Singapore arrests three in AI chip fraud case amid US crackdown on exports
item.description: Singapore authorities have charged three men in connection with an alleged fraud scheme involving the illicit procurement of Nvidia’s high-end AI chips. The men, two Singaporeans and one Chinese national, are accused of misrepresenting end-user details to acquire the advanced hardware.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight
item.description: AI presents opportunities for greater efficiency, but GAO chief data officer says its effectiveness hinges on data quality. The post AI & Data Exchange 2025: GAO’s Lindsey Saul on AI helping speed data analytics, improve oversight first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple, Samsung strike deal for purchase of San Jose office building
item.description: Samsung has bought from Apple a big office building in San Jose, a deal that would enable the tech and consumer electronics titan to widen its Silicon Valley footprint.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: What is an AI server? Why artificial intelligence needs specialized systems
item.description: AI servers are advanced computing systems designed to handle complex, resource-intensive AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Prioridades de los líderes TI en materia de IA: productividad por encima de la innovación
item.description: Los líderes de TI siguen apuntando a lo más fácil cuando utilizan la inteligencia artificial (IA), centrándose en mejorar la productividad de los empleados en lugar de dar rienda suelta a usos más avanzados como permitir la innovación o aumentar los ingresos, según una nueva encuesta.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI priorities: IT leaders emphasize productivity over innovation
item.description: IT leaders are still aiming for the low-hanging fruit when using AI, zeroing in on improving employee productivity rather than more advanced uses such as enabling innovation or expanding revenue, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The rising threat of shadow AI
item.description: Employees in a large financial organization began developing AI tools to automate time-consuming tasks such as weekly report generation. They didn’t think about what could go wrong. Within a few months, unauthorized applications skyrocketed from just a couple to 65.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI unveils its new GPT-4.5 large language model
item.description: OpenAI released a new base model on Thursday called GPT-4.5, which the company said is its best and smartest model for chat yet. It’s not a reasoning model like OpenAI’s o1 and o3 models, but it can be used to train other models to be reasoning models.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: This new bill aims to make presidential meme coins illegal
item.description: California Democrat Rep. Sam Liccardo, a freshman congressman who represents Silicon Valley, said he’s surprised the first piece of legislation he’s sponsoring takes aim at President Donald Trump’s meme coin.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Overcoming data compliance and security challenges in the age of AI
item.description: We are in the era of artificial intelligence (AI), and businesses are unlocking unprecedented opportunities for growth and efficiency.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DeepSeek offers steep discounts, escalating AI price war
item.description: Chinese AI firm DeepSeek has unveiled a significant price reduction for developers using its AI models, a move that could intensify competition among both domestic and global rivals.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Meet the AI real estate agent making millions
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malware exposes 3.9 billion passwords in huge cybersecurity threat
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips on how you can protect your devices against infostealer malware, which was used to infect 4.3 million machines last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 ways to make Windows 11 work like Windows 10
item.description: Switching to Windows 11? You’re not alone. With the clock ticking down to Windows 10’s end-of-support date in October, professionals around the globe are making plans — and lots of them are making the upgrade to Windows 11.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Autonomous robotaxi successfully completes 75 mph highway testing
item.description: Motional is developing autonomous vehicles capable of handling a wide range of real-world scenarios. Its high-speed robotaxi testing reached speeds of 75 mph.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
